joined_reference: it confirms fincher 's status as a film maker who artfully bends technical know-how to the service of psychological insight .  too much of it feels unfocused and underdeveloped .  a great ensemble cast ca n't lift this heartfelt enterprise out of the familiar .  prurient playthings aside , there 's little to love about this english trifle .  it moves quickly , adroitly , and without fuss ; it does n't give you time to reflect on the inanity -- and the cold war datedness -- of its premise .  it proves quite compelling as an intense , brooding character study .  bogdanovich tantalizes by offering a peep show into the lives of the era 's creme de la celluloid .  looks and feels like a project better suited for the small screen .  verbinski implements every hack-artist trick to give us the ooky-spookies .  this is a good script , good dialogue , funny even for adults .  a painfully funny ode to bad behavior .  there is a fabric of complex ideas here , and feelings that profoundly deepen them .  the overall effect is less like a children 's movie than a recruitment film for future hollywood sellouts .  as the two leads , lathan and diggs are charming and have chemistry both as friends and lovers .  the title not only describes its main characters , but the lazy people behind the camera as well .  zhang ... has done an amazing job of getting realistic performances from his mainly nonprofessional cast .  not only is undercover brother as funny , if not more so , than both austin powers films , but it 's also one of the smarter , savvier spoofs to come along in some time .  beautifully observed , miraculously unsentimental comedy-drama .  the last 20 minutes are somewhat redeeming , but most of the movie is the same teenage american road-trip drek we 've seen before - only this time you have to read the fart jokes  if you dig on david mamet 's mind tricks ... rent this movie and enjoy !  for all its impressive craftsmanship , and despite an overbearing series of third-act crescendos , lily chou-chou never really builds up a head of emotional steam .  yes , dull .  shaky close-ups of turkey-on-rolls , stubbly chins , liver spots , red noses and the filmmakers new bobbed do draw easy chuckles but lead nowhere .  the second coming of harry potter is a film far superior to its predecessor .  if you 're hard up for raunchy college humor , this is your ticket right here .  nonsensical , dull `` cyber-horror '' flick is a grim , hollow exercise in flat scares and bad acting .  a delectable and intriguing thriller filled with surprises , read my lips is an original .  thanks to scott 's charismatic roger and eisenberg 's sweet nephew , roger dodger is one of the most compelling variations on in the company of men .  there 's no emotional pulse to solaris .  a fast , funny , highly enjoyable movie .  too restrained to be a freak show , too mercenary and obvious to be cerebral , too dull and pretentious to be engaging ... the isle defies an easy categorization .  an unwise amalgam of broadcast news and vibes .  a pleasant enough romance with intellectual underpinnings , the kind of movie that entertains even as it turns maddeningly predictable .  it 's not original , and , robbed of the element of surprise , it does n't have any huge laughs in its story of irresponsible cops who love to play pranks .  it 's hard to imagine alan arkin being better than he is in this performance .  dazzles with its fully-written characters , its determined stylishness ( which always relates to characters and story ) and johnny dankworth 's best soundtrack in years .  fun , flip and terribly hip bit of cinematic entertainment .  the movie fails to live up to the sum of its parts .  one of the more intelligent children 's movies to hit theaters this year .  corpus collosum -- while undeniably interesting -- wore out its welcome well before the end credits rolled about 45 minutes in .  the performances take the movie to a higher level .  one of those pictures whose promising , if rather precious , premise is undercut by amateurish execution .  a compelling spanish film about the withering effects of jealousy in the life of a young monarch whose sexual passion for her husband becomes an obsession .  uses high comedy to evoke surprising poignance .  it has all the excitement of eating oatmeal .  feature debuter d.j. caruso directs a crack ensemble cast , bringing screenwriter tony gayton 's narcotics noir to life .  the old-world - meets-new mesh is incarnated in the movie 's soundtrack , a joyful effusion of disco bollywood that , by the end of monsoon wedding , sent my spirit soaring out of the theater .  an important movie , a reminder of the power of film to move us and to make us examine our values .  it 's a bit disappointing that it only manages to be decent instead of dead brilliant .  it 's not the ultimate depression-era gangster movie .  it has the charm of the original american road movies , feasting on the gorgeous , ramshackle landscape of the filmmaker 's motherland .  the vitality of the actors keeps the intensity of the film high , even as the strafings blend together .  the movie is n't just hilarious : it 's witty and inventive , too , and in hindsight , it is n't even all that dumb .  it 's difficult to imagine the process that produced such a script , but here 's guessing that spray cheese and underarm noises played a crucial role .  while locals will get a kick out of spotting cleveland sites , the rest of the world will enjoy a fast-paced comedy with quirks that might make the award-winning coen brothers envious .  broomfield turns his distinctive ` blundering ' style into something that could really help clear up the case .  oh come on .  awesome creatures , breathtaking scenery , and epic battle scenes add up to another ` spectacular spectacle . '  paid in full is so stale , in fact , that its most vibrant scene is one that uses clips from brian de palma 's scarface .  warm water under a red bridge is a quirky and poignant japanese film that explores the fascinating connections between women , water , nature , and sexuality .  the wild thornberrys movie is a jolly surprise .  the film flat lines when it should peak and is more missed opportunity and trifle than dark , decadent truffle .  for the first time in years , de niro digs deep emotionally , perhaps because he 's been stirred by the powerful work of his co-stars .  escaping the studio , piccoli is warmly affecting and so is this adroitly minimalist movie .  a moody , multi-dimensional love story and sci-fi mystery , solaris is a thought-provoking , haunting film that allows the seeds of the imagination to germinate .  a celebration of quirkiness , eccentricity , and certain individuals ' tendency to let it all hang out , and damn the consequences .  harris commands the screen , using his frailty to suggest the ravages of a life of corruption and ruthlessness .  it does nothing new with the old story , except to show fisticuffs in this sort of stop-go slow motion that makes the gang rumbles look like they 're being streamed over a 28k modem .  martin and barbara are complex characters -- sometimes tender , sometimes angry -- and the delicate performances by sven wollter and viveka seldahl make their hopes and frustrations vivid .  as a first-time director , paxton has tapped something in himself as an actor that provides frailty with its dark soul .  the result is a gaudy bag of stale candy , something from a halloween that died .  it is great summer fun to watch arnold and his buddy gerald bounce off a quirky cast of characters .  the best revenge may just be living well because this film , unlike other dumas adaptations , is far more likened to a treasure than a lengthy jail sentence .  sticky sweet sentimentality , clumsy plotting and a rosily myopic view of life in the wwii-era mississippi delta undermine this adaptation .  even if you do n't think ( kissinger 's ) any more guilty of criminal activity than most contemporary statesmen , he 'd sure make a courtroom trial great fun to watch .  forced , familiar and thoroughly condescending .  this is a winning ensemble comedy that shows canadians can put gentle laughs and equally gentle sentiments on the button , just as easily as their counterparts anywhere else in the world .  scores no points for originality , wit , or intelligence .  an entertaining , colorful , action-filled crime story with an intimate heart .  manages to be sweet and wickedly satisfying at the same time .  i got a headache watching this meaningless downer .  it 's a stunning lyrical work of considerable force and truth .  i 've always dreamed of attending cannes , but after seeing this film , it 's not that big a deal .  so much facile technique , such cute ideas , so little movie .  the movie is dawn of the dead crossed with john carpenter 's ghosts of mars , with zombies not as ghoulish as the first and trains not as big as the second .  just not campy enough  when leguizamo finally plugged an irritating character late in the movie .  visually imaginative , thematically instructive and thoroughly delightful , it takes us on a roller-coaster ride from innocence to experience without even a hint of that typical kiddie-flick sentimentality .  ( e ) ventually , every idea in this film is flushed down the latrine of heroism .  the story and structure are well-honed .  for close to two hours the audience is forced to endure three terminally depressed , mostly inarticulate , hyper dysfunctional families for the price of one .  a rarity among recent iranian films : it 's a comedy full of gentle humor that chides the absurdity of its protagonist 's plight .  / but daphne , you 're too buff / fred thinks he 's tough / and velma - wow , you 've lost weight !  `` mostly martha '' is a bright , light modern day family parable that wears its heart on its sleeve for all to see .  a bloated gasbag thesis grotesquely impressed by its own gargantuan aura of self-importance ...  too slow , too long and too little happens .  not only are the special effects and narrative flow much improved , and daniel radcliffe more emotionally assertive this time around as harry , but the film conjures the magic of author j.k. rowling 's books .  has all the depth of a wading pool .  even in its most tedious scenes , russian ark is mesmerizing .  on this tricky topic , tadpole is very much a step in the right direction , with its blend of frankness , civility and compassion .  candid and comfortable ; a film that deftly balances action and reflection as it lets you grasp and feel the passion others have for their work .  this is human comedy at its most amusing , interesting and confirming .  in an effort , i suspect , not to offend by appearing either too serious or too lighthearted , it offends by just being wishy-washy .  you wonder why enough was n't just a music video rather than a full-length movie .  this is not the undisputed worst boxing movie ever , but it 's certainly not a champion - the big loser is the audience .  ... a fun little timewaster , helped especially by the cool presence of jean reno .  mcconaughey 's fun to watch , the dragons are okay , not much fire in the script .  there is nothing outstanding about this film , but it is good enough and will likely be appreciated most by sailors and folks who know their way around a submarine .  care deftly captures the wonder and menace of growing up , but he never really embraces the joy of fuhrman 's destructive escapism or the grace-in-rebellion found by his characters .  less dizzying than just dizzy , the jaunt is practically over before it begins .  a coarse and stupid gross-out .  serving sara does n't serve up a whole lot of laughs .  writer/director joe carnahan 's grimy crime drama is a manual of precinct cliches , but it moves fast enough to cover its clunky dialogue and lapses in logic .  this is an egotistical endeavor from the daughter of horror director dario argento ( a producer here ) , but her raw performance and utter fearlessness make it strangely magnetic .  the movie does a good job of laying out some of the major issues that we encounter as we journey through life .  a hamfisted romantic comedy that makes our girl the hapless facilitator of an extended cheap shot across the mason-dixon line .  sustains its dreamlike glide through a succession of cheesy coincidences and voluptuous cheap effects , not the least of which is rebecca romijn-stamos .  it inspires a continuing and deeply satisfying awareness of the best movies as monumental ` picture shows . '  nothing is sacred in this gut-buster .  it 's hampered by a lifetime-channel kind of plot and a lead actress who is out of her depth .  and the lesson , in the end , is nothing new .  aside from minor tinkering , this is the same movie you probably loved in 1994 , except that it looks even better .  brilliantly explores the conflict between following one 's heart and following the demands of tradition .  a rigorously structured and exquisitely filmed drama about a father and son connection that is a brief shooting star of love .  it can not be enjoyed , even on the level that one enjoys a bad slasher flick , primarily because it is dull .  a taut psychological thriller that does n't waste a moment of its two-hour running time .  a study in shades of gray , offering itself up in subtle plot maneuvers ...  the far future may be awesome to consider , but from period detail to matters of the heart , this film is most transporting when it stays put in the past .

joined_reference: if the first men in black was money , the second is small change .  charles ' entertaining film chronicles seinfeld 's return to stand-up comedy after the wrap of his legendary sitcom , alongside wannabe comic adams ' attempts to get his shot at the big time .  if the movie succeeds in instilling a wary sense of ` there but for the grace of god , ' it is far too self-conscious to draw you deeply into its world .  ... designed to provide a mix of smiles and tears , `` crossroads '' instead provokes a handful of unintentional howlers and numerous yawns .  whether you like rap music or loathe it , you ca n't deny either the tragic loss of two young men in the prime of their talent or the power of this movie .  with tightly organized efficiency , numerous flashbacks and a constant edge of tension , miller 's film is one of 2002 's involvingly adult surprises .  the minor figures surrounding ( bobby ) ... form a gritty urban mosaic .  vera 's three actors -- mollà , gil and bardem -- excel in insightful , empathetic performances .  it 's great escapist fun that recreates a place and time that will never happen again .  the son 's room is a triumph of gentility that earns its moments of pathos .  but the power of these ( subjects ) is obscured by the majority of the film that shows a stationary camera on a subject that could be mistaken for giving a public oration , rather than contributing to a film 's narrative .  it 's one pussy-ass world when even killer-thrillers revolve around group therapy sessions .  stealing harvard aspires to comedic grand larceny but stands convicted of nothing more than petty theft of your time .  light years / several warp speeds / levels and levels of dilithium crystals better than the pitiful insurrection .  the director knows how to apply textural gloss , but his portrait of sex-as-war is strictly sitcom .  so , too , is this comedy about mild culture clashing in today 's new delhi .  deliriously funny , fast and loose , accessible to the uninitiated , and full of surprises  filmmakers who can deftly change moods are treasures and even marvels .  although laced with humor and a few fanciful touches , the film is a refreshingly serious look at young women .  so devoid of any kind of intelligible story that it makes films like xxx and collateral damage seem like thoughtful treatises  the film is beautifully mounted , but , more to the point , the issues are subtly presented , managing to walk a fine line with regard to the question of joan 's madness .  makes for a pretty unpleasant viewing experience .  late marriage 's stiffness is unlikely to demonstrate the emotional clout to sweep u.s. viewers off their feet .  no sophomore slump for director sam mendes , who segues from oscar winner to oscar-winning potential with a smooth sleight of hand .  it 's too self-important and plodding to be funny , and too clipped and abbreviated to be an epic .  collateral damage finally delivers the goods for schwarzenegger fans .  ... mafia , rap stars and hood rats butt their ugly heads in a regurgitation of cinematic violence that gives brutal birth to an unlikely , but likable , hero . '  detox is ultimately a pointless endeavor .  nicks , seemingly uncertain what 's going to make people laugh , runs the gamut from stale parody to raunchy sex gags to formula romantic comedy .  the band 's courage in the face of official repression is inspiring , especially for aging hippies ( this one included ) .  the mesmerizing performances of the leads keep the film grounded and keep the audience riveted .  all that 's missing is the spontaneity , originality and delight .  portentous and pretentious , the weight of water is appropriately titled , given the heavy-handedness of it drama .  outer-space buffs might love this film , but others will find its pleasures intermittent .  offers very little genuine romance and even fewer laughs ... a sad sitcom of a movie , largely devoid of charm .  every nanosecond of the the new guy reminds you that you could be doing something else far more pleasurable .  suffocated by its fussy script and uptight characters , this musty adaptation is all the more annoying since it 's been packaged and sold back to us by hollywood .  its well of thorn and vinegar ( and simple humanity ) has long been plundered by similar works featuring the insight and punch this picture so conspicuously lacks .  i had to look away - this was god awful .  drops you into a dizzying , volatile , pressure-cooker of a situation that quickly snowballs out of control , while focusing on the what much more than the why .  it all adds up to good fun .  at its best , queen is campy fun like the vincent price horror classics of the '60s .  to say this was done better in wilder 's some like it hot is like saying the sun rises in the east .  when the film ended , i felt tired and drained and wanted to lie on my own deathbed for a while .  green might want to hang onto that ski mask , as robbery may be the only way to pay for his next project .  a film about a young man finding god that is accessible and touching to the marrow .  this flick is about as cool and crowd-pleasing as a documentary can get .  although huppert 's intensity and focus has a raw exhilaration about it , the piano teacher is anything but fun .  despite its title , punch-drunk love is never heavy-handed .  movie fans , get ready to take off ... the other direction .  i 'll bet the video game is a lot more fun than the film .  a beguiling splash of pastel colors and prankish comedy from disney .  a romantic comedy enriched by a sharp eye for manners and mores .  made with no discernible craft and monstrously sanctimonious in dealing with childhood loss .  the humor is forced and heavy-handed , and occasionally simply unpleasant .  not since tom cruise in risky business has an actor made such a strong impression in his underwear .  while the ideas about techno-saturation are far from novel , they 're presented with a wry dark humor .  thanks to haynes ' absolute control of the film 's mood , and buoyed by three terrific performances , far from heaven actually pulls off this stylistic juggling act .  may be far from the best of the series , but it 's assured , wonderfully respectful of its past and thrilling enough to make it abundantly clear that this movie phenomenon has once again reinvented itself for a new generation .  the film 's welcome breeziness and some unbelievably hilarious moments -- most portraying the idiocy of the film industry -- make it mostly worth the trip .  nelson 's brutally unsentimental approach ... sucks the humanity from the film , leaving behind an horrific but weirdly unemotional spectacle .  a coda in every sense , the pinochet case splits time between a minute-by-minute account of the british court 's extradition chess game and the regime 's talking-head survivors .  ` easily my choice for one of the year 's best films . '  a spellbinding african film about the modern condition of rootlessness , a state experienced by millions around the globe .  or doing last year 's taxes with your ex-wife .  impostor has a handful of thrilling moments and a couple of good performances , but the movie does n't quite fly .  a nightmare date with a half-formed wit done a great disservice by a lack of critical distance and a sad trust in liberal arts college bumper sticker platitudes .  the chateau cleverly probes the cross-cultural differences between gauls and yanks .  his last movie was poetically romantic and full of indelible images , but his latest has nothing going for it .  at its worst , it implodes in a series of very bad special effects .  chokes on its own depiction of upper-crust decorum .  displaying about equal amounts of naiveté , passion and talent , beneath clouds establishes sen as a filmmaker of considerable potential .  this time mr. burns is trying something in the martin scorsese street-realist mode , but his self-regarding sentimentality trips him up again .  the sort of film that makes me miss hitchcock , but also feel optimistic that there 's hope for popular cinema yet .  allows us to hope that nolan is poised to embark a major career as a commercial yet inventive filmmaker .  stealing harvard is evidence that the farrelly bros. -- peter and bobby -- and their brand of screen comedy are wheezing to an end , along with green 's half-hearted movie career .  a by-the-numbers patient/doctor pic that covers all the usual ground  if you 've ever entertained the notion of doing what the title of this film implies , what sex with strangers actually shows may put you off the idea forever .  it 's so good that its relentless , polished wit can withstand not only inept school productions , but even oliver parker 's movie adaptation .  by getting myself wrapped up in the visuals and eccentricities of many of the characters , i found myself confused when it came time to get to the heart of the movie .  harrison 's flowers puts its heart in the right place , but its brains are in no particular place at all .  this surreal gilliam-esque film is also a troubling interpretation of ecclesiastes .  nothing in waking up in reno ever inspired me to think of its inhabitants as anything more than markers in a screenplay .  the story and the friendship proceeds in such a way that you 're watching a soap opera rather than a chronicle of the ups and downs that accompany lifelong friendships .  the film will play equally well on both the standard and giant screens .  the movie 's accumulated force still feels like an ugly knot tightening in your stomach .  few films capture so perfectly the hopes and dreams of little boys on baseball fields as well as the grown men who sit in the stands .  comes off like a rejected abc afterschool special , freshened up by the dunce of a screenwriting 101 class .  it 's of the quality of a lesser harrison ford movie - six days , seven nights , maybe , or that dreadful sabrina remake .  a lackluster , unessential sequel to the classic disney adaptation of j.m. barrie 's peter pan .  the entire movie is about a boring , sad man being boring and sad .  the film 's hackneyed message is not helped by the thin characterizations , nonexistent plot and pretentious visual style .  this is a train wreck of an action film -- a stupefying attempt by the filmmakers to force-feed james bond into the mindless xxx mold and throw 40 years of cinematic history down the toilet in favor of bright flashes and loud bangs .  feels too formulaic and too familiar to produce the transgressive thrills of early underground work .  there are simply too many ideas floating around -- part farce , part sliding doors , part pop video -- and yet failing to exploit them .  yakusho and shimizu ... create engaging characterizations in imamura 's lively and enjoyable cultural mix .  a swashbuckling tale of love , betrayal , revenge and above all , faith .  we have n't seen such hilarity since say it is n't so !  attempts by this ensemble film to impart a message are so heavy-handed that they instead pummel the audience .  a very long movie , dull in stretches , with entirely too much focus on meal preparation and igloo construction .  like mike is a winner for kids , and no doubt a winner for lil bow wow , who can now add movies to the list of things he does well .  the tale of tok ( andy lau ) , a sleek sociopath on the trail of o ( takashi sorimachi ) , the most legendary of asian hitmen , is too scattershot to take hold .  a tv style murder mystery with a few big screen moments ( including one that seems to be made for a different film altogether ) .  may reawaken discussion of the kennedy assassination but this fictional film looks made for cable rather than for the big screen .  a superbly acted and funny/gritty fable of the humanizing of one woman at the hands of the unseen forces of fate .  a tender , heartfelt family drama .  while its careful pace and seemingly opaque story may not satisfy every moviegoer 's appetite , the film 's final scene is soaringly , transparently moving .  birthday girl is an amusing joy ride , with some surprisingly violent moments .  for anyone unfamiliar with pentacostal practices in general and theatrical phenomenon of hell houses in particular , it 's an eye-opener .  it 's a grab bag of genres that do n't add up to a whole lot of sense .  something akin to a japanese alice through the looking glass , except that it seems to take itself far more seriously .  that dogged good will of the parents and ` vain ' jia 's defoliation of ego , make the film touching despite some doldrums .  visually rather stunning , but ultimately a handsome-looking bore , the true creativity would have been to hide treasure planet entirely and completely reimagine it .  almost gags on its own gore .  the characters are interesting and often very creatively constructed from figure to backstory .  not only unfunny , but downright repellent .  this is wild surreal stuff , but brilliant and the camera just kind of sits there and lets you look at this and its like you 're going from one room to the next and none of them have any relation to the other .  this is a story of two misfits who do n't stand a chance alone , but together they are magnificent .  at the very least , if you do n't know anything about derrida when you walk into the theater , you wo n't know much more when you leave .  his comedy premises are often hackneyed or just plain crude , calculated to provoke shocked laughter , without following up on a deeper level .  the moviegoing equivalent of going to a dinner party and being forced to watch the host and hostess 's home video of their baby 's birth .  trite , banal , cliched , mostly inoffensive .  a warm but realistic meditation on friendship , family and affection .  majidi is an unconventional storyteller , capable of finding beauty in the most depressing places .  there is no pleasure in watching a child suffer .  expect the same-old , lame-old slasher nonsense , just with different scenery .  without non-stop techno or the existential overtones of a kieslowski morality tale , maelström is just another winter sleepers .  woody allen 's latest is an ambling , broad comedy about all there is to love -- and hate -- about the movie biz .

joined_reference: a breezy romantic comedy that has the punch of a good sitcom , while offering exceptionally well-detailed characters .  ... takes the beauty of baseball and melds it with a story that could touch anyone regardless of their familiarity with the sport  i can take infantile humor ... but this is the sort of infantile that makes you wonder about changing the director and writer 's diapers .  very psychoanalytical -- provocatively so -- and also refreshingly literary .  it 's a scattershot affair , but when it hits its mark it 's brilliant .  a strangely compelling and brilliantly acted psychological drama .  it 's a bad thing when a movie has about as much substance as its end credits blooper reel .  one of the most significant moviegoing pleasures of the year .  this one is definitely one to skip , even for horror movie fanatics .  in execution , this clever idea is far less funny than the original , killers from space .  the notion that bombing buildings is the funniest thing in the world goes entirely unexamined in this startlingly unfunny comedy .  sit through this one , and you wo n't need a magic watch to stop time ; your dvd player will do it for you .  pumpkin means to be an outrageous dark satire on fraternity life , but its ambitions far exceed the abilities of writer adam larson broder and his co-director , tony r. abrams , in their feature debut .  a woman 's pic directed with resonance by ilya chaiken .  it 's a cookie-cutter movie , a cut-and-paste job .  preaches to two completely different choirs at the same time , which is a pretty amazing accomplishment .  morton uses her face and her body language to bring us morvern 's soul , even though the character is almost completely deadpan .  even with a green mohawk and a sheet of fire-red flame tattoos covering his shoulder , however , kilmer seems to be posing , rather than acting .  funny but perilously slight .  ramsay , as in ratcatcher , remains a filmmaker with an acid viewpoint and a real gift for teasing chilly poetry out of lives and settings that might otherwise seem drab and sordid .  sacrifices the value of its wealth of archival foot-age with its less-than-objective stance .  determined to be fun , and bouncy , with energetic musicals , the humor did n't quite engage this adult .  a sometimes tedious film .  due to some script weaknesses and the casting of the director 's brother , the film trails off into inconsequentiality .  a very well-made , funny and entertaining picture .  one of creepiest , scariest movies to come along in a long , long time , easily rivaling blair witch or the others .  fancy a real downer ?  a warm , funny , engaging film .  the lion king was a roaring success when it was released eight years ago , but on imax it seems better , not just bigger .  the best that can be said about the work here of scottish director ritchie ... is that he obviously does n't have his heart in it .  affleck and jackson are good sparring partners .  the socio-histo-political treatise is told in earnest strides ... ( and ) personal illusion is deconstructed with poignancy .  unlike the speedy wham-bam effect of most hollywood offerings , character development -- and more importantly , character empathy -- is at the heart of italian for beginners .  bennett 's naturalistic performance speaks volumes more truth than any ` reality ' show , and anybody contemplating their own drastic life changes should watch some body first .  scooby dooby doo / and shaggy too / you both look and sound great .  ... is an arthritic attempt at directing by callie khouri .  worth watching for dong jie 's performance -- and for the way it documents a culture in the throes of rapid change .  his healthy sense of satire is light and fun ...  this illuminating documentary transcends our preconceived vision of the holy land and its inhabitants , revealing the human complexities beneath .  a quiet , pure , elliptical film  too often , the viewer is n't reacting to humor so much as they are wincing back in repugnance .  based on a devilishly witty script by heather mcgowan and niels mueller , the film gets great laughs , but never at the expense of its characters  ... a hollow joke told by a cinematic gymnast having too much fun embellishing the misanthropic tale to actually engage it .  what was once original has been co-opted so frequently that it now seems pedestrian .  because of an unnecessary and clumsy last scene , ` swimfan ' left me with a very bad feeling .  a giggle-inducing comedy with snappy dialogue and winning performances by an unlikely team of oscar-winners : susan sarandon and goldie hawn .  seldom has a movie so closely matched the spirit of a man and his work .  what really makes it special is that it pulls us into its world , gives us a hero whose suffering and triumphs we can share , surrounds him with interesting characters and sends us out of the theater feeling we 've shared a great adventure .  it 's another stale , kill-by-numbers flick , complete with blade-thin characters and terrible , pun-laden dialogue .  while it 's genuinely cool to hear characters talk about early rap records ( sugar hill gang , etc. ) , the constant referencing of hip-hop arcana can alienate even the savviest audiences .  for the most part , director anne-sophie birot 's first feature is a sensitive , extraordinarily well-acted drama .  a fitfully amusing romp that , if nothing else , will appeal to fans of malcolm in the middle and its pubescent star , frankie muniz .  a tender , witty , captivating film about friendship , love , memory , trust and loyalty .  one of the best films of the year with its exploration of the obstacles to happiness faced by five contemporary individuals ... a psychological masterpiece .  it ca n't decide if it wants to be a mystery/thriller , a romance or a comedy .  a giggle a minute .  this piece of channel 5 grade trash is , quite frankly , an insult to the intelligence of the true genre enthusiast .  mattei is tiresomely grave and long-winded , as if circularity itself indicated profundity .  it 's a beautiful madness .  ... the movie is just a plain old monster .  writer-director 's mehta 's effort has tons of charm and the whimsy is in the mixture , the intoxicating masala , of cultures and film genres .  an absurdist comedy about alienation , separation and loss .  though it 's become almost redundant to say so , major kudos go to leigh for actually casting people who look working-class .  schaeffer has to find some hook on which to hang his persistently useless movies , and it might as well be the resuscitation of the middle-aged character .  it 's like watching a nightmare made flesh .  k-19 exploits our substantial collective fear of nuclear holocaust to generate cheap hollywood tension .  ( a ) n utterly charming and hilarious film that reminded me of the best of the disney comedies from the 60s .  and if you 're not nearly moved to tears by a couple of scenes , you 've got ice water in your veins .  ... the film suffers from a lack of humor ( something needed to balance out the violence ) ...  i sympathize with the plight of these families , but the movie does n't do a very good job conveying the issue at hand .  there has always been something likable about the marquis de sade .  i thought my own watch had stopped keeping time as i slogged my way through clockstoppers .  corny , schmaltzy and predictable , but still manages to be kind of heartwarming , nonetheless .  it has its moments of swaggering camaraderie , but more often just feels generic , derivative and done to death .  as the latest bid in the tv-to-movie franchise game , i spy makes its big-screen entry with little of the nervy originality of its groundbreaking small-screen progenitor .  two hours fly by -- opera 's a pleasure when you do n't have to endure intermissions -- and even a novice to the form comes away exhilarated .  a good piece of work more often than not .  villeneuve spends too much time wallowing in bibi 's generic angst ( there are a lot of shots of her gazing out windows ) .  i just loved every minute of this film .  delivers the same old same old , tarted up with latin flava and turned out by hollywood playas .  director uwe boll and the actors provide scant reason to care in this crude '70s throwback .  this movie is something of an impostor itself , stretching and padding its material in a blur of dead ends and distracting camera work .  slapstick buffoonery can tickle many a preschooler 's fancy , but when it costs a family of four about $ 40 to see a film in theaters , why spend money on a dog like this when you can rent a pedigree instead ?  a full world has been presented onscreen , not some series of carefully structured plot points building to a pat resolution .  although german cooking does not come readily to mind when considering the world 's best cuisine , mostly martha could make deutchland a popular destination for hungry tourists .  i do n't think i laughed out loud once .  a string of rehashed sight gags based in insipid vulgarity .  this movie is maddening .  the script is n't very good ; not even someone as gifted as hoffman ( the actor ) can make it work .  one of those energetic surprises , an original that pleases almost everyone who sees it .  it 's one of those baseball pictures where the hero is stoic , the wife is patient , the kids are as cute as all get-out and the odds against success are long enough to intimidate , but short enough to make a dream seem possible .  it haunts you , you ca n't forget it , you admire its conception and are able to resolve some of the confusions you had while watching it .  ( næs ) directed the stage version of elling , and gets fine performances from his two leads who originated the characters on stage .  you wo n't like roger , but you will quickly recognize him .  it 's made with deftly unsettling genre flair .  we know the plot 's a little crazy , but it held my interest from start to finish .  having had the good sense to cast actors who are , generally speaking , adored by the movie-going public , khouri then gets terrific performances from them all .  a simple , but gritty and well-acted ensemble drama that encompasses a potent metaphor for a country still dealing with its fascist past .  for all its technical virtuosity , the film is so mired in juvenile and near-xenophobic pedagogy that it 's enough to make one pine for the day when godard can no longer handle the rigors of filmmaking .  the affectionate loopiness that once seemed congenital to demme 's perspective has a tough time emerging from between the badly dated cutesy-pie mystery scenario and the newfangled hollywood post-production effects .  ( t ) here 's only so much anyone can do with a florid , overplotted , anne rice rock 'n' roll vampire novel before the built-in silliness of the whole affair defeats them .  a disappointment for those who love alternate versions of the bard , particularly ones that involve deep fryers and hamburgers .  byler reveals his characters in a way that intrigues and even fascinates us , and he never reduces the situation to simple melodrama .  it takes a strange kind of laziness to waste the talents of robert forster , anne meara , eugene levy , and reginald veljohnson all in the same movie .  i do n't mind having my heartstrings pulled , but do n't treat me like a fool .  holm ... embodies the character with an effortlessly regal charisma .  the subtle strength of `` elling '' is that it never loses touch with the reality of the grim situation .  utterly lacking in charm , wit and invention , roberto benigni 's pinocchio is an astonishingly bad film .  the end result is a film that 's neither .  it 's a lovely film with lovely performances by buy and accorsi .  rare birds has more than enough charm to make it memorable .  in all , this is a watchable movie that 's not quite the memorable experience it might have been .  it treats women like idiots .  it made me want to wrench my eyes out of my head and toss them at the screen .  burns never really harnesses to full effect the energetic cast .  it takes a certain kind of horror movie to qualify as ` worse than expected , ' but ghost ship somehow manages to do exactly that .  does n't offer much besides glib soullessness , raunchy language and a series of brutal set pieces ... that raise the bar on stylized screen violence .  all the amped-up tony hawk-style stunts and thrashing rap-metal ca n't disguise the fact that , really , we 've been here , done that .  no aspirations to social import inform the movie version .  instead , he shows them the respect they are due .  taylor appears to have blown his entire budget on soundtrack rights and had nothing left over for jokes .  uses sharp humor and insight into human nature to examine class conflict , adolescent yearning , the roots of friendship and sexual identity .  with the exception of some fleetingly amusing improvisations by cedric the entertainer as perry 's boss , there is n't a redeeming moment here .  do not see this film .  a gorgeous , high-spirited musical from india that exquisitely blends music , dance , song , and high drama .  the acting , costumes , music , cinematography and sound are all astounding given the production 's austere locales .  ( director ) o'fallon manages to put some lovely pictures up on the big screen , but his skill at telling a story -- he also contributed to the screenplay -- falls short .  rarely has so much money delivered so little entertainment .

joined_reference: should have been someone else -  ( t ) his beguiling belgian fable , very much its own droll and delicate little film , has some touching things to say about what is important in life and why .  whether writer-director anne fontaine 's film is a ghost story , an account of a nervous breakdown , a trip down memory lane , all three or none of the above , it is as seductive as it is haunting .  a psychological thriller with a genuinely spooky premise and an above-average cast , actor bill paxton 's directing debut is a creepy slice of gothic rural americana .  cq 's reflection of artists and the love of cinema-and-self suggests nothing less than a new voice that deserves to be considered as a possible successor to the best european directors .  coughs and sputters on its own postmodern conceit .  the film 's tone and pacing are off almost from the get-go .  you 'll gasp appalled and laugh outraged and possibly , watching the spectacle of a promising young lad treading desperately in a nasty sea , shed an errant tear .  michael gerbosi 's script is economically packed with telling scenes .  in exactly 89 minutes , most of which passed as slowly as if i 'd been sitting naked on an igloo , formula 51 sank from quirky to jerky to utter turkey .  huston nails both the glad-handing and the choking sense of hollow despair .  holden caulfield did it better .  jason x is positively anti-darwinian : nine sequels and 400 years later , the teens are none the wiser and jason still kills on auto-pilot .  my reaction in a word : disappointment .  ... a story we have n't seen on the big screen before , and it 's a story that we as americans , and human beings , should know .  a working class `` us vs. them '' opera that leaves no heartstring untugged and no liberal cause unplundered .  it 's fascinating to see how bettany and mcdowell play off each other .  plays like a volatile and overlong w magazine fashion spread .  the most hopelessly monotonous film of the year , noteworthy only for the gimmick of being filmed as a single unbroken 87-minute take .  american chai encourages rueful laughter at stereotypes only an indian-american would recognize .  crackerjack entertainment -- nonstop romance , music , suspense and action .  bad .  a by-the-numbers effort that wo n't do much to enhance the franchise .  if steven soderbergh 's ` solaris ' is a failure it is a glorious failure .  intriguing documentary which is emotionally diluted by focusing on the story 's least interesting subject .  one of the more irritating cartoons you will see this , or any , year .  the film is based on truth and yet there is something about it that feels incomplete , as if the real story starts just around the corner .  directed in a paint-by-numbers manner .  though perry and hurley make inspiring efforts to breathe life into the disjointed , haphazard script by jay scherick and david ronn , neither the actors nor director reginald hudlin can make it more than fitfully entertaining .  samira makhmalbaf 's new film blackboards is much like the ethos of a stream of consciousness , although , it 's unfortunate for the viewer that the thoughts and reflections coming through are torpid and banal  it wants to tweak them with a taste of tangy new humor .  the heavy-handed film is almost laughable as a consequence .  among the year 's most intriguing explorations of alientation .  the jabs it employs are short , carefully placed and dead-center .  it 's so mediocre , despite the dynamic duo on the marquee , that we just ca n't get no satisfaction .  it just may inspire a few younger moviegoers to read stevenson 's book , which is a treasure in and of itself .  good old-fashioned slash-and-hack is back !  here 's yet another studio horror franchise mucking up its storyline with glitches casual fans could correct in their sleep .  a misogynistic piece of filth that attempts to pass itself off as hip , young adult entertainment .  as unseemly as its title suggests .  the film 's few ideas are stretched to the point of evaporation ; the whole central section is one big chase that seems to have no goal and no urgency .  for each chuckle there are at least 10 complete misses , many coming from the amazingly lifelike tara reid , whose acting skills are comparable to a cardboard cutout .  not since japanese filmmaker akira kurosawa 's ran have the savagery of combat and the specter of death been visualized with such operatic grandeur .  fresnadillo 's dark and jolting images have a way of plying into your subconscious like the nightmare you had a week ago that wo n't go away .  jose campanella delivers a loosely autobiographical story brushed with sentimentality but brimming with gentle humor , bittersweet pathos , and lyric moments that linger like snapshots of memory .  moody , heartbreaking , and filmed in a natural , unforced style that makes its characters seem entirely convincing even when its script is not .  a movie that reminds us of just how exciting and satisfying the fantasy cinema can be when it 's approached with imagination and flair .  there 's too much falseness to the second half , and what began as an intriguing look at youth fizzles into a dull , ridiculous attempt at heart-tugging .  cool ?  an occasionally funny , but overall limp , fish-out-of-water story .  it 's a charming and often affecting journey .  it 's inoffensive , cheerful , built to inspire the young people , set to an unending soundtrack of beach party pop numbers and aside from its remarkable camerawork and awesome scenery , it 's about as exciting as a sunburn .  generally , clockstoppers will fulfill your wildest fantasies about being a different kind of time traveler , while happily killing 94 minutes .  if there 's one thing this world needs less of , it 's movies about college that are written and directed by people who could n't pass an entrance exam .  another in-your-face wallow in the lower depths made by people who have never sung those blues .  moretti 's compelling anatomy of grief and the difficult process of adapting to loss .  confirms the nagging suspicion that ethan hawke would be even worse behind the camera than he is in front of it .  basically a static series of semi-improvised ( and semi-coherent ) raps between the stars .  slick piece of cross-promotion .  one of the smartest takes on singles culture i 've seen in a long time .  it provides an honest look at a community striving to anchor itself in new grounds .  or emptying rat traps .  a valueless kiddie paean to pro basketball underwritten by the nba .  a literate presentation that wonderfully weaves a murderous event in 1873 with murderous rage in 2002 .  a sequel that 's much too big for its britches .  it 's hard to like a film about a guy who is utterly unlikeable , and shiner , starring michael caine as an aging british boxing promoter desperate for a taste of fame and fortune , is certainly that .  hit and miss as far as the comedy goes and a big ole ' miss in the way of story .  the terrific and bewilderingly underrated campbell scott gives a star performance that is nothing short of mesmerizing .  while ( hill ) has learned new tricks , the tricks alone are not enough to salvage this lifeless boxing film .  kinnear does n't aim for our sympathy , but rather delivers a performance of striking skill and depth .  a poignant , artfully crafted meditation on mortality .  it 's fun lite .  excessive , profane , packed with cartoonish violence and comic-strip characters .  without ever becoming didactic , director carlos carrera expertly weaves this novelistic story of entangled interrelationships and complex morality .  a dumb movie with dumb characters doing dumb things and you have to be really dumb not to see where this is going .  a smart , witty follow-up .  macdowell , whose wifty southern charm has anchored lighter affairs ... brings an absolutely riveting conviction to her role .  i am sorry that i was unable to get the full brunt of the comedy .  if you can stomach the rough content , it 's worth checking out for the performances alone .  the film is quiet , threatening and unforgettable .  a synthesis of cliches and absurdities that seems positively decadent in its cinematic flash and emptiness .  ... turns so unforgivably trite in its last 10 minutes that anyone without a fortified sweet tooth will likely go into sugar shock .  for the most part , it 's a work of incendiary genius , steering clear of knee-jerk reactions and quick solutions .  enormously entertaining for moviegoers of any age .  the very definition of the ` small ' movie , but it is a good stepping stone for director sprecher .  my thoughts were focused on the characters .  looking aristocratic , luminous yet careworn in jane hamilton 's exemplary costumes , rampling gives a performance that could not be improved upon . '  but taken as a stylish and energetic one-shot , the queen of the damned can not be said to suck .  what the director ca n't do is make either of val kilmer 's two personas interesting or worth caring about .  it 's just disappointingly superficial -- a movie that has all the elements necessary to be a fascinating , involving character study , but never does more than scratch the surface .  every time you look , sweet home alabama is taking another bummer of a wrong turn .  this nickleby thing might have more homosexual undertones than an eddie murphy film .  neither parker nor donovan is a typical romantic lead , but they bring a fresh , quirky charm to the formula .  good car chases , great fight scenes , and a distinctive blend of european , american and asian influences .  building slowly and subtly , the film , sporting a breezy spontaneity and realistically drawn characterizations , develops into a significant character study that is both moving and wise .  teen movies have really hit the skids .  by candidly detailing the politics involved in the creation of an extraordinary piece of music , ( jones ) calls our attention to the inherent conflict between commerce and creativity .  leigh 's film is full of memorable performances from top to bottom .  the film 's performances are thrilling .  a densely constructed , highly referential film , and an audacious return to form that can comfortably sit among jean-luc godard 's finest work .  it showcases carvey 's talent for voices , but not nearly enough and not without taxing every drop of one 's patience to get to the good stuff .  the cold turkey would 've been a far better title .  what is 100 % missing here is a script of even the most elemental literacy , an inkling of genuine wit , and anything resembling acting .  the volatile dynamics of female friendship is the subject of this unhurried , low-key film that is so off-hollywood that it seems positively french in its rhythms and resonance .  ( lawrence bounces ) all over the stage , dancing , running , sweating , mopping his face and generally displaying the wacky talent that brought him fame in the first place .  this is a shameless sham , calculated to cash in on the popularity of its stars .  something like scrubbing the toilet .  a must-see for the david mamet enthusiast and for anyone who appreciates intelligent , stylish moviemaking .  despite all evidence to the contrary , this clunker has somehow managed to pose as an actual feature movie , the kind that charges full admission and gets hyped on tv and purports to amuse small children and ostensible adults .  it feels like an after-school special gussied up with some fancy special effects , and watching its rote plot points connect is about as exciting as gazing at an egg timer for 93 minutes .  it deserves to be seen by anyone with even a passing interest in the events shaping the world beyond their own horizons .  with its dogged hollywood naturalism and the inexorable passage of its characters toward sainthood , windtalkers is nothing but a sticky-sweet soap .  a subject like this should inspire reaction in its audience ; the pianist does not .  no telegraphing is too obvious or simplistic for this movie .  but it could have been worse .  for movie lovers as well as opera lovers , tosca is a real treat .  griffiths proves she 's that rare luminary who continually raises the standard of her profession .  what better message than ` love thyself ' could young women of any size receive ?  reggio 's continual visual barrage is absorbing as well as thought-provoking .  partway through watching this saccharine , easter-egg-colored concoction , you realize that it is made up of three episodes of a rejected tv show .  seldahl 's barbara is a precise and moving portrait of someone whose world is turned upside down , first by passion and then by illness .  the character of zigzag is not sufficiently developed to support a film constructed around him .  if director michael dowse only superficially understands his characters , he does n't hold them in contempt .  this is so bad .  chilling , well-acted , and finely directed : david jacobson 's dahmer .  a deep and meaningful film .  in its best moments , resembles a bad high school production of grease , without benefit of song .  it seems like i have been waiting my whole life for this movie and now i ca n't wait for the sequel .

joined_reference: director of photography benoit delhomme shot the movie in delicious colors , and the costumes and sets are grand .  an effectively creepy , fear-inducing ( not fear-reducing ) film from japanese director hideo nakata , who takes the superstitious curse on chain letters and actually applies it .  more whiny downer than corruscating commentary .  from the opening scenes , it 's clear that all about the benjamins is a totally formulaic movie .  as surreal as a dream and as detailed as a photograph , as visually dexterous as it is at times imaginatively overwhelming .  bleakly funny , its characters all the more touching for refusing to pity or memorialize themselves .  it 's a work by an artist so in control of both his medium and his message that he can improvise like a jazzman .  this film seems thirsty for reflection , itself taking on adolescent qualities .  if your taste runs to ` difficult ' films you absolutely ca n't miss it .  part low rent godfather .  will amuse and provoke adventurous adults in specialty venues .  a solid examination of the male midlife crisis .  it 's a much more emotional journey than what shyamalan has given us in his past two movies , and gibson , stepping in for bruce willis , is the perfect actor to take us on the trip .  it haunts , horrifies , startles and fascinates ; it is impossible to look away .  audrey tatou has a knack for picking roles that magnify her outrageous charm , and in this literate french comedy , she 's as morning-glory exuberant as she was in amélie .  in the end , we are left with something like two ships passing in the night rather than any insights into gay love , chinese society or the price one pays for being dishonest .  that 's pure pr hype .  no one but a convict guilty of some truly heinous crime should have to sit through the master of disguise .  every dance becomes about seduction , where backstabbing and betrayals are celebrated , and sex is currency .  sometimes seems less like storytelling than something the otherwise compelling director needed to get off his chest .  try as i may , i ca n't think of a single good reason to see this movie , even though everyone in my group extemporaneously shouted , ` thank you ! '  sam mendes has become valedictorian at the school for soft landings and easy ways out .  the most compelling wiseman epic of recent years .  unfortunately , it 's not silly fun unless you enjoy really bad movies .  the x potion gives the quickly named blossom , bubbles and buttercup supernatural powers that include extraordinary strength and laser-beam eyes , which unfortunately do n't enable them to discern flimsy screenplays .  richard gere and diane lane put in fine performances as does french actor oliver martinez .  for the most part stevens glides through on some solid performances and witty dialogue .  offers much to enjoy ... and a lot to mull over in terms of love , loyalty and the nature of staying friends .  first-time writer-director serry shows a remarkable gift for storytelling with this moving , effective little film .  it 's just filler .  not far beneath the surface , this reconfigured tale asks disturbing questions about those things we expect from military epics .  in a way , the film feels like a breath of fresh air , but only to those that allow it in .  it offers little beyond the momentary joys of pretty and weightless intellectual entertainment .  that 's a cheat .  it takes talent to make a lifeless movie about the most heinous man who ever lived .  very bad .  rarely has leukemia looked so shimmering and benign .  the emotions are raw and will strike a nerve with anyone who 's ever had family trauma .  lapaglia 's ability to convey grief and hope works with weaver 's sensitive reactions to make this a two-actor master class .  has a lot of the virtues of eastwood at his best .  given how heavy-handed and portent-heavy it is , this could be the worst thing soderbergh has ever done .  like being trapped at a perpetual frat party ... how can something so gross be so boring ?  if looking for a thrilling sci-fi cinematic ride , do n't settle for this imposter .  vera 's technical prowess ends up selling his film short ; he smoothes over hard truths even as he uncovers them .  while undisputed is n't exactly a high , it is a gripping , tidy little movie that takes mr. hill higher than he 's been in a while .  a rewarding work of art for only the most patient and challenge-hungry moviegoers .  it 's as if you 're watching a movie that was made in 1978 but not released then because it was so weak , and it has been unearthed and released now , when it has become even weaker .  far more imaginative and ambitious than the trivial , cash-in features nickelodeon has made from its other animated tv series .  the action switches between past and present , but the material link is too tenuous to anchor the emotional connections that purport to span a 125-year divide .  this is n't even madonna 's swept away .  immersing us in the endlessly inventive , fiercely competitive world of hip-hop djs , the project is sensational and revelatory , even if scratching makes you itch .  a quiet treasure -- a film to be savored .  instead of hiding pinocchio from critics , miramax should have hidden it from everyone .  this movie seems to have been written using mad-libs .  ( w ) hile long on amiable monkeys and worthy environmentalism , jane goodall 's wild chimpanzees is short on the thrills the oversize medium demands .  but this films lacks the passion required to sell the material .  the movie is what happens when you blow up small potatoes to 10 times their natural size , and it ai n't pretty .  so unremittingly awful that labeling it a dog probably constitutes cruelty to canines .  there 's a wickedly subversive bent to the best parts of birthday girl .  but it still jingles in the pocket .  my wife is an actress is an utterly charming french comedy that feels so american in sensibility and style it 's virtually its own hollywood remake .  instead of a hyperbolic beat-charged urban western , it 's an unpretentious , sociologically pointed slice of life .  you really have to wonder how on earth anyone , anywhere could have thought they 'd make audiences guffaw with a script as utterly diabolical as this .  like watching a dress rehearsal the week before the show goes up : everything 's in place but something 's just a little off-kilter .  dragonfly has no atmosphere , no tension -- nothing but costner , flailing away .  a wildly inconsistent emotional experience .  the man from elysian fields is a cold , bliss-less work that groans along thinking itself some important comment on how life throws us some beguiling curves .  a better title , for all concerned , might be swept under the rug .  there seems to be no clear path as to where the story 's going , or how long it 's going to take to get there .  the so-inept - it 's - surreal dubbing ( featuring the voices of glenn close , regis philbin and breckin meyer ) brings back memories of cheesy old godzilla flicks .  a difficult , absorbing film that manages to convey more substance despite its repetitions and inconsistencies than do most films than are far more pointed and clear .  big fat waste of time .  there 's really only one good idea in this movie , but the director runs with it and presents it with an unforgettable visual panache .  there 's not enough here to justify the almost two hours .  there is n't nearly enough fun here , despite the presence of some appealing ingredients .  of course , by more objective measurements it 's still quite bad .  puts a human face on a land most westerners are unfamiliar with .  a gripping movie , played with performances that are all understated and touching .  it 's one heck of a character study -- not of hearst or davies but of the unique relationship between them .  i 'm just too bored to care .  the humor is n't as sharp , the effects not as innovative , nor the story as imaginative as in the original .  comes ... uncomfortably close to coasting in the treads of the bicycle thief .  sade is an engaging look at the controversial eponymous and fiercely atheistic hero .  a big , gorgeous , sprawling swashbuckler that delivers its diversions in grand , uncomplicated fashion .  as vulgar as it is banal .  it 's a buggy drag .  smart , provocative and blisteringly funny .  the experience of going to a film festival is a rewarding one ; the experiencing of sampling one through this movie is not .  there are plot holes big enough for shamu the killer whale to swim through .  you do n't have to know about music to appreciate the film 's easygoing blend of comedy and romance .  how do you spell cliché ?  the weight of the piece , the unerring professionalism of the chilly production , and the fascination embedded in the lurid topic prove recommendation enough .  it 's clear the filmmakers were n't sure where they wanted their story to go , and even more clear that they lack the skills to get us to this undetermined destination .  if you believe any of this , i can make you a real deal on leftover enron stock that will double in value a week from friday .  entertains by providing good , lively company .  overall the film feels like a low-budget tv pilot that could not find a buyer to play it on the tube .  passable entertainment , but it 's the kind of motion picture that wo n't make much of a splash when it 's released , and will not be remembered long afterwards .  it is amusing , and that 's all it needs to be .  in the end , the movie collapses on its shaky foundation despite the best efforts of director joe carnahan .  it 's like every bad idea that 's ever gone into an after-school special compiled in one place , minus those daytime programs ' slickness and sophistication ( and who knew they even had any ? ) .  like you could n't smell this turkey rotting from miles away .  travels a fascinating arc from hope and euphoria to reality and disillusionment .  it has charm to spare , and unlike many romantic comedies , it does not alienate either gender in the audience .  overall very good for what it 's trying to do .  once the 50 year old benigni appears as the title character , we find ourselves longing for the block of wood to come back .  not the kind of film that will appeal to a mainstream american audience , but there is a certain charm about the film that makes it a suitable entry into the fest circuit .  ... nothing scary here except for some awful acting and lame special effects .  a cartoon that 's truly cinematic in scope , and a story that 's compelling and heartfelt -- even if the heart belongs to a big , four-legged herbivore .  the talented and clever robert rodriguez perhaps put a little too much heart into his first film and did n't reserve enough for his second .  about a manga-like heroine who fights back at her abusers , it 's energetic and satisfying if not deep and psychological .  still , as a visual treat , the film is almost unsurpassed .  a lean , deftly shot , well-acted , weirdly retro thriller that recalls a raft of '60s and '70s european-set spy pictures .  well-nigh unendurable ... though the picture strains to become cinematic poetry , it remains depressingly prosaic and dull .  just one bad idea after another .  some of their jokes work , but most fail miserably and in the end , pumpkin is far more offensive than it is funny .  liotta put on 30 pounds for the role , and has completely transformed himself from his smooth , goodfellas image .  pacino is brilliant as the sleep-deprived dormer , his increasing weariness as much existential as it is physical .  turns potentially forgettable formula into something strangely diverting .  it 's a demented kitsch mess ( although the smeary digital video does match the muddled narrative ) , but it 's savvy about celebrity and has more guts and energy than much of what will open this year .  highbrow self-appointed guardians of culture need not apply , but those who loved cool as ice have at last found a worthy follow-up .  a solid film ... but more conscientious than it is truly stirring .  ultimately feels empty and unsatisfying , like swallowing a communion wafer without the wine .  for starters , the story is just too slim .  nine queens is not only than a frighteningly capable debut and genre piece , but also a snapshot of a dangerous political situation on the verge of coming to a head .  ... with `` the bourne identity '' we return to the more traditional action genre .  at a time when half the so-called real movies are little more than live-action cartoons , it 's refreshing to see a cartoon that knows what it is , and knows the form 's history .  it 's refreshing to see a girl-power movie that does n't feel it has to prove anything .  old-form moviemaking at its best .
